claim: Tools available only to one agent shall live under that agent's tools/ subdirectory (domains/<domain>/agents/<name>/tools/).
why: Counterpart to R-shared-tools-in-spec-tools -- private scope. In the Go port, the private/shared tool boundary is conceptual: no agents/ directories exist yet in any domain (R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent: sub-agent crystals materialize only at real spawn time), so the constraint is vacuously satisfied. The registered enforcer check_agent_has_tools_subdir (internal/invariants/domain_structure.go) is an HONEST NO-OP (returns nil unconditionally): it checks a FILE SYSTEM STRUCTURE property (every agent directory carries a tools/ subdir), but the Go invariant contract (Check func(*ontology.Graph) []Violation) operates on an already-loaded in-memory graph and has no access to the agents directory path on disk -- filesystem-coherence checks belong to a separate agent-scaffolding layer outside internal/invariants. FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): enforcement is ENFORCED with a no-op enforcer that cannot verify the claim because it operates on the in-memory graph, not the filesystem. The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch.
